title: acaena × eglochidiata
intro: Acaena × eglochidiata, also known as silver button or white kitten, is a hybrid plant species within the Acaena genus. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial native to New Zealand, known for its small, round silver-white leaves. Acaena × eglochidiata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including skin irritations and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or as a poultice to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations, providing relief from minor health issues such as eczema and acne.
